channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Gentrace makes it easier for businesses to test AI-powered software
item.description: As businesses continue to integrate generative AI into their products, many find it challenging to actually test whether the AI is behaving correctly and giving useful answers.
item.dc:creator: Steven Melendez, Fast Company

item.title: How Big Tech labor organizers aim to unite for Trump 2.0
item.description: Donald Trump’s first electoral victory forever changed the culture inside big tech firms. What had been nascent worker organizing efforts turned into mass movements to protest their companies’ politics, environmental practices, treatment of contract workers, handling of harassment cases, and more.
item.dc:creator: Sean Captain, Fast Company

item.title: How AI orchestration has become more important than the models themselves
item.description: Large language models (LLMs) just keep getting better. In just about two years since OpenAI jolted the news cycle with the introduction of ChatGPT, we’ve already seen the launch and subsequent upgrades of dozens of competing models.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Johnson pours cold water on KOSA push
item.description: House Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) cautioned Republicans against rushing to pass a bill to protect kids and teens online on Tuesday as the GOP prepares to take control of the White House and Congress.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Study: Scientists make more discoveries with help from AI
item.description: A new study by a PhD student at MIT indicates that AI tools can help scientists make more discoveries, according to Nature. In the study by Aidan Toner-Rodgers, an unspecified laboratory in the US with 1,018 researchers used an unspecified custom machine-learning tool.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Fortnite players 'tricked' into unwanted purchases are starting to get refunds. Here's how to apply
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — U.S. consumers who were “tricked” into purchases they didn't want from Fortnite maker Epic Games are now starting to receive refund checks, the Federal Trade Commission said this week.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Boosting Cyprus' defense is key so close to the war-wracked Mideast, defense minister says
item.description: Cyprus' defense minister says that bolstering the defense capacity of the east Mediterranean island nation by getting new armaments is critical for a country so close to the war-wracked Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Menelaos Hadjicostis, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Russia disconnects several regions from the global internet to test its sovereign net
item.description: Not even VPNs managed to help Russians bypass internet restrictions in what looks like a "new phase of online censorship" for the country.
item.dc:creator: chiara.castro@futurenet.com (Chiara Castro), TechRadar

item.title: Sora AI Video Generator Released, but Human Depictions Limited by OpenAI
item.description: OpenAI releases Sora video generator, limits human depictions due to misuse concerns
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: 5 Lessons every CFO can learn from a billionaire singer-songwriter
item.description: When you have a young daughter like I do, it’s almost impossible to avoid getting swept up into the Taylor Swift fan frenzy.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Unlocking AI potential: The essential role of hybrid, multi-cloud strategies
item.description: At NetApp, we recognize that AI is not merely a technological tool—it’s a transformative mindset that can reshape organizations and industries. To harness its full potential, it is essential to cultivate a data-driven culture that permeates every level of your company.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Quantum Computing: A Game-Changer for Organizations and Industries
item.description: United Nations designation of quantum computing as a key area for development, impact on finance, IT professionals' need to adapt, and emerging field opportunities.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Unlocking the Power of PDFs: Reliable, Secure, and Flexible Document Sharing
item.description: Discover the benefits and versatility of Portable Document Format (PDF) files in today's digital landscape.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Best Places to Work in IT 2025: Top Organizations Excel in Employee Retention and Engagement
item.description: Discover the best places to work in IT 2025, highlighting organizations that excel in employee retention, engagement, remote/hybrid work, benefits, and IT career development.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: IT at Tokio Marine means continuous learning — and many hats
item.description: The insurance industry is known for being slow and steady, but at Tokio Marine North America Services (TMNAS), a dogged emphasis on growth, diversity of thought, and continuous learning has helped accelerate an IT culture predicated on innovation and career mobility.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: At OCLC, technology with a purpose drives workplace diversity
item.description: OCLC may be a midsize software provider catering to a niche industry, but it’s beating a lot of industry giants when it comes to workforce diversity.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Total Quality Logistics: Building a ‘talent destination’ through employee engagement
item.description: Don’t talk about industry slowdowns to Total Quality Logistics (TQL), which has made its largest-ever technology investments over the past three years to bring greater agility and resilience to the business.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Social media influencer is a growing career choice. Colleges have noticed
item.description: Have influencers on social media created a new career field worthy of formal education? A growing number of universities are looking at classes and majors aimed at giving influencers the skills needed to spot misinformation, communicate effectively with audiences and understand business contracts t
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, The Hill

item.title: What CIOs are in for with the EU’s Data Act
item.description: Over the course of this year, CIOs have spent time studying the Data Act, the European digital regulatory framework composed of a set of laws united by the aim to encourage innovation in European companies, and to open up new markets.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Qué esperar de la IA en la esfera empresarial en 2025
item.description: Este año, el entusiasmo inicial por la inteligencia artificial (IA) parece haberse calmado; de hecho, se han y establecido expectativas más realistas.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: 7 Strategies for Building High-Quality Chatbots
item.description: Discover how to craft exceptional conversational experiences with AI using scripted responses, conversation starters, and more.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Nvidia's stock dips after China opens probe of the AI chip company for violating anti-monopoly laws
item.description: Shares of Nvidia fell Monday after China said it is investigating the high-flying U.S. microchip company over suspected violations of Chinese anti-monopoly laws.
item.dc:creator: MATT OTT, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Python a shoo-in for Tiobe language of the year
item.description: High-flying Python is expected to win the Tiobe programming language of the year designation for 2024, as the language with the biggest ratings increase in Tiobe’s language popularity index during the year. Python also ranks as the most popular programming language in the index overall.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: California to consider requiring mental health warnings on social media sites
item.description: SACRAMENTO, Calif. (AP) — California, home to some of the largest technology companies in the world, would be the first U.S. state to require mental health warning labels on social media sites if lawmakers pass a bill introduced Monday.
item.dc:creator: TRÂN NGUYỄN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Extreme looks to tame complexity with AI-powered platform for network and security management
item.description: Extreme Networks is rolling out an AI-infused networking platform that aims to help enterprise customers integrate, manage and automate a broad range of network and security technologies.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: CompTIA courts IT newcomers with ‘on-ramp’ network, security courses
item.description: New learning resources from CompTIA are aimed at helping entry-level IT professionals to accelerate their career path to networking and cybersecurity roles.
item.dc:creator: Network World
